title: introduce podman machine
body:
podman machine allows podman to create, manage, and interact with a vm
running some form of linux (default is fcos).  podman is then configured
to be able to interact with the vm automatically.

while this is usable on linux, the real push is to get this working on
both current apple architectures in macos.

Ashley Cui contributed to this PR and was a great help.

[NO TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Re-introducing podman-machine, eh ?

Good for you, saw it finally got FCOS...

But I got: `Error: exec: "qemu-kvm": executable file not found in $PATH`

Think the wrapper (for qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm) is called `kvm` on Ubuntu

----

Seems to be working, otherwise.

```console
$ ./bin/podman machine ssh myvm
Connecting to vm myvm. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
The authenticity of host '[localhost]:40765 ([127.0.0.1]:40765)' can't be established.
ECDSA key fingerprint is SHA256:R/ecwO+jyYE9Q1QI7D6x1RbZtGqzP1F099K0WN873Po.
Are you sure you want to continue connecting (yes/no/[fingerprint])? yes
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:40765' (ECDSA) to the list of known hosts.
Fedora CoreOS 33.20210315.1.0
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/c/server/coreos/

[core@localhost ~]$ podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Built:        Fri Feb 19 16:56:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[core@localhost ~]$ exit
logout
Connection to localhost closed.
$ podman-remote --connection myvm version

Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0

Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

Slightly bigger OS, at 1.8 GB.

~~https://github.com/boot2podman/boot2podman-fedora-iso~~
This should not be merged until podman 3.1 is released.
We are hoping to work with the FCOS team to shrink the size, IE Remove Moby, Containerd, Runc ...

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
First observation: please remove the merge commit. Don't need to squash down to 1, but there should not be a merge in the branch.

> We are hoping to work with the FCOS team to shrink the size, IE Remove Moby, Containerd, Runc ...

To be fair, the actual download/template is only 543M since it uses .xz compression on the .qcow2

But it was a bit surprising to me that Fedora CoreOS image would be bigger than the Fedora image

Not sure the average user cares if it is 300M or 600M.

But if they do, one could do a Fedora alternative ISO ?

OK I am going to merge, We can continue to churn on this and further clean it up.
/lgtm
